stimulus: Storyteller Jason Reynolds's verse novel Long Way Down, winner of the 2018 Newbery Honor for Children's Literature, is composed of ______blank protagonist, fifteen-year-old Will Holloman.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. Nothing belongs to the "vignettes" in the sentence, so it should not be possessive—just a simple plural noun. The protagonist does belong to the book—it's the protagonist of the book—so "book" needs to be a singular possessive noun.

Choice A is incorrect. This choice uses the singular possessive "vignette's," but the text indicates that it should be the simple plural "vignettes": there is more than one vignette, and nothing belongs to the vignettes. This choice also uses the plural possessive "books'," which is incorrect because there is only one book. Choice C is incorrect. This choice uses the singular possessive "vignette's," but the text indicates that it should be the simple plural "vignettes." Choice D is incorrect. This choice uses the plural possessive "books'," which is incorrect because there is only one book, so it should be the singular possessive "book's."
